==================================================
PROJECT INSTRUCTIONS FILE  
Fullstack Global Master Development, Security, Architecture, and Best-Practice Rulebook  
Updated for Late-2025 Standards 
NEVER WRITE TO THIS FILE DIRECTLY UNLESS INSTRUCTED BY USER
==================================================

Core Priorities  
maximum security  
maximum correctness  
maximum maintainability  
maximum clarity  
modern production standards  
scalability and performance  
observability and monitoring  
AI safety  
OWASP 2025 alignment  
never truncate  
always generate full implementations  
adapt architecture intelligently  
no extra md files when working on project unless aked for, just edit the directories README.md as needed with overall function and not recent changes and then tell me yourself any changes you just made without an summary file
no making extra rules file for current directory unless I ask for it
never use emojis in code

==================================================
0. UNIVERSAL INTELLIGENCE AND ADAPTATION RULE
==================================================

Before writing any code, determine exactly what the project requires.  
No unnecessary backend, database, authentication, or security layers.

Include backend only when:  
project explicitly fullstack  
server logic required  
persistent data required  
authentication required  

Include authentication only when:  
project includes login  
authentication is explicitly requested  

Include database only when:  
persistent storage required  
project type depends on it  

Apply high-security architecture only when:  
production  
sensitive data  
explicit request  

Simple demos avoid unnecessary layers.  
Revamps require full modernization, restructuring, security alignment, and updated documentation.

==================================================
1. GLOBAL DEVELOPMENT PRINCIPLES
==================================================

Secure, readable, maintainable code  
Do not commit secrets  
Use environment variables  
Default database is MySQL  
Use parameterized SQL only  
Frontend never accesses database  
Backend exposes REST or GraphQL  
Architecture must be layered and modular  
Clarity has priority  
Use TypeScript for production  
Use ESM imports  
Use linting and formatting tools  
Use static analysis and dependency scanning  
Maintain software bill of materials  
Version APIs when scaling  
Modular monolith is acceptable until microservices required  

==================================================
2. FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE (REACT DEFAULT)
==================================================

Recommended stack  
React functional components  
TypeScript  
React Router  
axios or fetch  
Bulma or CSS modules  
Core React hooks  

Modern React 2025 features  
React Server Components  
Server side rendering  
Streaming server rendering  
Suspense based data fetching  
Concurrent rendering  
Transitions and deferred values  
Dynamic import code splitting  
Modern bundlers  

State management hierarchy  
Use React built-ins first  
Then Zustand, Jotai, or React Query  
Use Redux Toolkit only when unavoidable  

Performance  
Memoization when needed  
Bundle and image optimization  
Avoid prop drilling  
Use CDNs  

Accessibility WCAG 2.2  
No obscured focus  
Visible focus indicators  
Accessible alternatives to dragging  
Minimum interactive target size  
Consistent help  
Minimal redundant entry  
Accessible authentication flows  

Internationalization  
Externalized text  
Localization libraries  
Locale-aware formatting  
RTL support  

==================================================
3. BACKEND ARCHITECTURE (NODE AND EXPRESS)
==================================================

Used only when necessary.

Folder structure  
routes  
controllers  
services  
middleware  
config  
db  
utils  

Backend requirements  
ESM imports  
express.json for parsing  
async and await  
route to controller to service to database pattern  
strict validation and sanitization  
centralized error handling  
structured logging  
security headers  
strict CORS  
rate limiting  
TypeScript recommended  

Optional  
WebSockets  
GraphQL  
gRPC  
background workers  

==================================================
4. DATABASE RULES (MYSQL)
==================================================

Use MySQL connection pool  
Use parameterized queries only  
Validate and sanitize all inputs  
Use least-privilege database accounts  
Do not use root for application code  
Require TLS for production  
Normalize schema unless intentionally denormalized  
Create proper indexes  
Enable slow query logs  
Use EXPLAIN for optimization  
Automate backups  
Test restore procedures  
Partition very large tables  
Use read replicas for scaling  

Encryption  
Encrypt in transit  
Encrypt sensitive fields at rest  

==================================================
5. SECURITY RULES
==================================================

Assume all input is malicious.

Mandatory safeguards  
sanitization  
validation  
output escaping  
secure cookies only  
CSRF defenses when needed  
HTTPS everywhere  
permission checks  
no sensitive logging  
rate limiting  
automated threat detection  

OWASP 2025 alignment  
injection prevention  
object property authorization protection  
function level authorization protection  
validation of external API responses  
resource consumption limits  
automated threat defense  
supply chain security  
secure design reviews  

Password hashing  
Argon2id preferred  
scrypt optional  
bcrypt only fallback  

==================================================
6. AUTHENTICATION AND AUTHORIZATION
==================================================

Include only when login exists.

Login flow  
receive login request  
validate credentials  
compare hashed password  
issue session or token in secure cookie  
validate protected routes  
logout clears cookie  
tokens rotated on login  

Cookie rules  
HttpOnly true  
Secure true in production  
SameSite Lax default  
SameSite None only when required  

Never store tokens in  
local storage  
session storage  
URLs  

Modern authentication additions  
short lived access tokens  
rotating refresh tokens  
multi factor authentication optional  
passkey and WebAuthn support  
session revocation  
server side logout invalidation  

==================================================
7. EXPRESS ROUTING AND MIDDLEWARE
==================================================

Request pipeline  
security headers  
json parsing  
CORS  
logging  
rate limiting  
validation  
authentication  
authorization  
controller  
service  
database  
response  

Required middleware  
json parser  
strict CORS rules  
security headers  
structured logging  
rate limiting  
validation layer  
global error handler  

==================================================
8. BACKEND SECURITY DEEP DIVE
==================================================

XSS protection  
sanitize  
escape  
HttpOnly cookies  

CSRF protection  
SameSite Lax default  
tokens when required  

CORS  
strict origin allowlist  
never wildcard with credentials  

SSRF protection  
URL allowlists  
never access internal resources from user data  

Security headers  
HSTS  
frame blocking  
content type enforcement  
referrer policy  
permissions policy  

==================================================
9. PROJECT STRUCTURE RULES
==================================================

File placement follows user instructions unless unspecified  
Do not generate extra documents  
Do not summarize unless asked  

README must include  
purpose  
installation  
usage  
environment configuration  
accurate architecture description  

==================================================
10. CODE COMMENT RULES
==================================================

Comments explain reasons, not mechanics  
No trivial comments  
Functions include docstrings  
Remove outdated comments  

==================================================
11. PERFORMANCE RULES
==================================================

Correctness first, clarity second, performance third  
Profile before optimizing  
Index query heavy tables  
Use Redis for caching  
Use CDNs for assets  
Use server side rendering for heavy pages  
Define service level objectives  
Monitor performance metrics  

==================================================
12. TESTING RULES
==================================================

Required tests  
unit tests  
integration tests  
end to end tests  
deterministic tests  
regression tests  
load and stress tests  

Testing tools  
Jest  
Testing Library  
Playwright or Cypress  
k6  

==================================================
13. CONTENT SECURITY POLICY RULES
==================================================

Apply only where needed  
script source restricted to self  
inline scripts require nonce  
minimal external script allowance  
block framing  

==================================================
14. PROJECT TYPE DECISION TREE
==================================================

Frontend only  
no backend  
no database  
no login  
no cookies  
no CORS  
no CSRF  

Fullstack without auth  
frontend  
backend  
database  
strict CORS  
no login or tokens  

Fullstack with auth  
all security layers  
role based authorization  
protected routes  
secure cookies  

Legacy revamp  
analyze entire project  
modernize  
fix security  
improve code layout  
preserve functionality  
update documentation  

==================================================
15. UNIVERSAL REVAMP RULE
==================================================

Whenever user requests improvement, optimization, modernization, enhancement, refactor, or rewrite  
perform full architecture inspection  
identify weaknesses  
apply all modern rules  
repair security  
repair database logic  
restructure folders  
update README  
preserve all functionality unless user states otherwise  

==================================================
16. UNIVERSAL SAFETY RULE
==================================================

Never expose secrets  
Never store tokens client side  
Never construct SQL strings  
Never allow unescaped html  
Never skip validation  
Never allow user controlled internal requests  
Never ignore OWASP guidelines  

==================================================
17. FULLSTACK DECISION SUPPORT
==================================================

Identify project type  
Include only required layers  
Update documentation when layers change  
Never truncate generated output  

==================================================
18. DEPLOYMENT AND DEVOPS RULES
==================================================

Use Docker  
Use minimal or distroless images  
Run containers as non root  
Use infrastructure as code  
Use continuous integration  
Scan dependencies  
Scan container images  
Sign build artifacts  
Maintain multiple environment configs  
Use autoscaling strategies  
Use health checks  
Set up alerting  
Plan disaster recovery  

==================================================
19. INTERNATIONALIZATION AND ACCESSIBILITY
==================================================

Internationalization  
externalize all strings  
use locale files  
handle pluralization  
support right to left layouts  
use locale aware formatting  

Accessibility WCAG 2.2  
keyboard accessible  
visible focus  
unobscured focus  
consistent help  
avoid duplicate information entry  
accessible authentication  
automated accessibility checks  

==================================================
20. AI AND EMERGING TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION
==================================================

AI safety  
sanitize all input  
escape risky characters  
avoid sending sensitive data  
use retrieval augmented generation for internal data  
rate limit AI endpoints  
log AI usage  
define guardrails  

Serverless  
use stateless functions  
ensure idempotency  
minimize cold start impact  
externalize state storage  
set strict resource limits  
monitor cost usage  

==================================================
21. OBSERVABILITY AND MONITORING
==================================================

Use OpenTelemetry for tracing and metrics  
Use Prometheus or Datadog dashboards  
Use structured logging  
Attach request identifiers  
Use centralized log aggregation  
Use error monitoring tools  
Set alerts on service level objective violations  
Use distributed tracing in multi service systems
